ocr.text: As AstraZeneca continues to enhance its position in the oncology sector, the performance of its drug portfolio remains a focal
point of strategic evaluation, particularly in light of recent manufacturing capacity expansions. The launch of DrugB, which has
generated an impressive £120M, exemplifies the robust demand for innovative cancer therapies.

Additionally, DrugC, designated as “Approved” in the monotherapy category, is projected to contribute £60M to overall
revenue. The ongoing investment in the oncology pipeline reflects AstraZeneca's commitment to addressing unmet medical
needs, with certain drugs, currently under development, estimated to yield significant returns; for example, one under review is
projected to bring in £90M.

In terms of expansion, the company plans to allocate £300M in CAPEX(GBP) to increase production capabilities, responding to
an anticipated production volume of 50 million units. This expansion is not only critical for meeting growing demand but also
aligns with AstraZeneca's broader strategy, as evidenced by a revenue growth rate of 15.3% anticipated from these investments.
Furthermore, the R&D investment remains a key driver for the future, with a significant allocation of £45M earmarked to
support the advancement of this promising portfolio. These metrics underscore AstraZeneca's proactive approach to solidifying
its leadership in the oncology market while ensuring sustainable growth.
